Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your customer information is accurate, accessible, and secure across all your devices and team members. Singapore SMEs need this to comply with PDPA regulations, enable remote and mobile sales teams, and integrate with accounting software like Xero or QuickBooks. A typical scenario: your sales rep on-site with a customer can instantly pull up the full contact history on their phone, or your finance team can automatically sync invoices to your accounting system without manual re-entry.

Sales Transactions.narrative: This family handles the core mechanics of selling—creating orders, managing prices, tracking commissions, and forecasting revenue. Singapore SMEs rely on this to process sales quickly, offer competitive pricing and promotions, and fairly compensate sales teams. A concrete example: a distributor can set volume discounts that automatically apply when a customer orders 100+ units, or a services firm can generate a custom quote for a bespoke package and track whether the deal will hit monthly quota.

CRM & Relationship.narrative: This family tracks every interaction with prospects and customers—calls, emails, meetings, notes—so no lead falls through the cracks and every team member knows the full history. Singapore SMEs benefit from structured lead qualification and pipeline visibility, which directly improves sales predictability and team accountability. For example, a B2B software reseller can automatically score inbound leads by company size and engagement, then route hot leads to the right account manager; or a consultant can log every client touchpoint and see at a glance which opportunities are stalled and need follow-up.

Marketing.narrative: This family automates customer outreach across email, social media, and web, and measures campaign performance so you know what drives sales. Singapore SMEs use this to nurture prospects cost-effectively, build brand presence on social platforms, and gather customer feedback to improve products. A typical scenario: a fintech startup runs an automated email nurture sequence triggered when a prospect downloads a whitepaper, while simultaneously monitoring Twitter mentions to respond to customer questions in real time.

Commerce.narrative: This family powers online selling—whether a B2C e-store, B2B ordering portal, marketplace presence, or loyalty program—and keeps inventory and customer identity consistent across all channels. Singapore SMEs selling online need this to compete with larger retailers, reduce manual order processing, and retain customers through rewards. A concrete example: a fashion brand sells on Shopee and Lazada from a single product catalogue with real-time inventory sync, or a F&B wholesaler offers a B2B portal where regular customers see negotiated prices and can order on net-30 terms.

Customer Service.narrative: This family organises customer support requests into tickets, routes them to the right person, tracks response times, and builds a knowledge base so customers can self-serve. Singapore SMEs use this to respond faster, reduce support costs, and improve customer satisfaction. For example, a SaaS company can automatically route billing questions to finance and technical issues to engineering, while publishing a searchable FAQ so 30% of questions are answered without human intervention.

Analytics.narrative: This family turns raw CRM data into dashboards, forecasts, and AI-driven insights—showing you which leads are most likely to convert, which customers might churn, and what to do next. Singapore SMEs use this to make faster, data-driven decisions and spot trends before competitors. A typical scenario: a sales manager sees a real-time dashboard showing pipeline by stage and win rate by product, or an e-commerce team uses churn prediction to identify at-risk customers and proactively offer a discount before they leave.
